mappings.rationale: Covers the engineering principles underlying vehicle design, ship construction, aircraft design, and road/bridge construction.
Directly relevant to the construction of roads, highways, bridges, tunnels, canals, and harbour works.
Covers the development of steam transportation including railroads and steamships, core topics in the section's history.
Covers navigational methods and systems relevant to both water and air transportation.
Relevant to ship design hydrodynamics and hydrostatics, and aerodynamic principles of aircraft flight.
Relevant to power units for propulsion including steam generators, internal-combustion engines, gas turbines, and nuclear reactors.
Relevant to the construction of railroad bridges, tunnels, and road infrastructure.
Covers the history of exploration that drove the development of ships, navigation, and transportation technology.
Relevant to the early development of sails, oars, and shipbuilding in water transportation.
Covers the history of overland transportation networks and their development across civilizations.
Relevant to the environmental impact of transportation systems and the development of sustainable transport.
Relevant to the environmental impact of transportation fuels and emissions from the transportation industry.
Relevant to water transportation, harbour works, and the maritime environment in which ships operate.
Relevant to the development of mass urban transport and traffic networks.
Covers the economic history of transportation development from primitive methods through modern air transport.
